note:
{
  "experiment_overview": "The experiment aims to investigate the effects of lipopolysaccharide (LPS) and adenosine triphosphate (ATP) on the death of PMA-differentiated THP-1 macrophages. The goal is to validate these effects through flow cytometry analysis.",
  "procedures_observed": [
    "A scientist in a lab coat and gloves adds serum-free medium to control tubes.",
    "The scientist then adds 5 μL of Annexin V-PE to one tube and 5 μL of 7-aminoactinomycin D to another tube.",
    "The tubes are incubated for 5 minutes.",
    "After incubation, the tubes are fixed using 2% glutaraldehyde and 100 mM phosphorous salts.",
    "The tubes are then centrifuged at 1,800 rpm for 10 minutes.",
    "The supernatant is removed, and the tubes are washed with PBS.",
    "The tubes are stained with propidium iodide and analyzed using flow cytometry."
  ],
  "materials_and_subjects": [
    "THP-1 macrophages",
    "Serum-free medium",
    "Annexin V-PE",
    "7-aminoactinomycin D",
    "Glutaraldehyde",
    "Phosphorous salts",
    "PBS (phosphate-buffered saline)",
    "Propidium iodide"
  ],
  "tools_and_setup": [
    "Test tubes",
    "Centrifuge",
    "Flow cytometer",
    "Lab bench with various equipment"
  ],
  "quantitative_observations": [
    "Volumes: 5 μL for Annexin V-PE and 7-aminoactinomycin D",
    "Time: 5 minutes for incubation",
    "Speed: 1,800 rpm
